Create an API key from https://buttondow: Create an API key from https://buttondown.com/keys and paste it here.

The email address of the subscriber.

Custom metadata to store on the subscrib: Custom metadata to store on the subscriber (JSON object).
ID of the subscriber who referred this c: ID of the subscriber who referred this contact.
The original IP address of the subscribe: The original IP address of the subscriber. Helps Buttondown evaluate spam.
Control how Buttondown handles duplicate: Control how Buttondown handles duplicate email addresses. Leave empty to keep the existing subscriber.
Set to true when the request originates: Set to true when the request originates from a trusted internal system without the subscriber's IP address.
Maximum number of subscribers to return: Maximum number of subscribers to return (1-1000).
Provide the cursor value from a previous: Provide the cursor value from a previous response to fetch the next page.
Filter by subscribers who have at least: Filter by subscribers who have at least one of these tags.
Return subscribers whose email address c: Return subscribers whose email address contains this value.
Write your email content in Markdown or: Write your email content in Markdown or HTML. Buttondown will detect the format automatically.
Choose how Buttondown should treat this: Choose how Buttondown should treat this email after creation.
Required when scheduling an email. Use I: Required when scheduling an email. Use ISO 8601 format.
Override the automatic slug used for the: Override the automatic slug used for the archive URL.
Custom metadata to attach to the email (: Custom metadata to attach to the email (JSON object).
IDs of attachments uploaded via the Butt: IDs of attachments uploaded via the Buttondown API.
Enable when this email should count agai: Enable when this email should count against pay-per-email billing for paid subscribers.
